segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vibrant green-to-blue gradient background displaying 'YOLTZIN HANDMADE' and 'SCRAPBOOK, CARDMAKING & PAPERCRAFTS'. Upbeat, light background music begins as a female voice, Galea, greets viewers, introducing herself and announcing that today's video will showcase some purchases she made at her favorite store, Acción. The scene then transitions to a static, top-down view of a brightly lit, cluttered crafting table, densely packed with a 'SCRAPBOOK HAUL' from 'Action Bélgica'. The table is filled with an array of colorful washi tapes, clear stamp sets with floral and leaf designs, bright buttons, a blue embossing folder, containers of rhinestones and other embellishments, and patterned paper packs labeled '32' and 'Craft folder'. Galea explains she visited Acción for two reasons: first, to find some papers for a friend, and second, because she had seen on social media that other crafters from the Netherlands had purchased new items, specifically mentioning a 'blog de die-cuts' and embellishments. She expresses disappointment that she didn't find the specific die-cut blog she was looking for but hopes to return soon, noting how beautiful the items looked in other videos.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vibrant, well-lit display of colorful scrapbooking supplies, including washi tapes, clear stamps with floral and leaf designs, colorful buttons, an embossing folder, rhinestones, and patterned paper packs, all arranged on a table. A light, upbeat instrumental background music begins as a female voice, speaking in Spanish, announces "Ahora sí, empiezo" (Now I start). She explains that she has checked and noted the prices on the packages because she often forgets them, ensuring she can tell viewers the exact cost, clarifying "cuánto me costó, perdón" (how much it cost me, sorry). She mentions she tries not to spend too much on scrapbooking materials, always looking for economical, functional, and good quality items, noting her consistent satisfaction with "productos de la Action" (Action products). A hand enters from the bottom left, gently touching and adjusting the embossing folder and a clear stamp sheet, indicating active engagement with the materials. The hand then picks up a package of washi tape, showcasing its design, before placing it back. The speaker then states, "Y comienzo con, bueno, comienzo con las washi tips" (And I start with, well, I start with the washi tapes), explaining she bought these recently as she hadn't gotten many on previous trips, despite there being many more designs and colors available. The hand selects and displays three different packages of washi tape—one with floral patterns, one fruit-themed, and one with geometric designs—holding them up for closer inspection and moving them to highlight their variety. The speaker confirms these "tres sets" (three sets) cost "80 centavos cada set" (80 cents each set). She specifically describes the fruit-themed set as "muy veraniego" (very summery), detailing its "piñas y y y sandías y el como limón" (pineapples, watermelons, and lemon) motifs. Text overlays like 'Action Bélgica' and 'YOLTZIN HANDMADE' are subtly present, contextualizing the retail source and the crafting content.

The background showcases a vibrant array of scrapbooking supplies. The hand then shifts focus to two clear plastic bags filled with colorful buttons, labeled 'Buttons' with quantities '52x' and '152x'. The voice mentions seeing other button colors like black, white, and grey, but states, "me traje estos estos cuatro colores" (I brought these these four colors), confirming the displayed buttons are the purchased ones. She further describes them as "rosas oscuros y claros" (dark and light pinks) among other colors. The voice also states, "Cada bolsita costaba 90 centavos" (Each little bag cost 90 cents). The well-lit, organized crafting table filled with supplies suggests a creative and engaging crafting environment throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of hands holding a clear plastic package labeled 'PINS AND IRON-ON PATCHES' against a backdrop of colorful scrapbooking supplies. A female voice explains in Spanish that these items are generally "para poner en la ropa" (for putting on clothes), though she personally won't be using them in that manner. The package, featuring a light green geometric pattern, contains five embroidered patches, which the speaker identifies as including two cactus designs, a llama, a red flower, and a sloth, noting she's unsure about one specific design. She also clarifies that the package contains "cuatro pins" (four pins). The hands gently rotate the package, allowing the viewer to see the details, then point to specific patches like the sloth, cactus, and llama, which the speaker reiterates. She reveals that she purchased two of these sets, each costing "12 euros." The scene transitions as the hands switch to a second package with a pink geometric background, also labeled 'PINS AND IRON-ON PATCHES,' which the speaker describes as being "de unicornio así esas cosas" (of unicorn and those things). This package contains different designs, including stars, a unicorn, a rainbow (which she refers to as a "nubecita" or little cloud), a heart, an envelope with a heart inside, and a blue diamond shape. The hands point to individual patches, emphasizing the unicorn, rainbow, and star designs, with the speaker noting that one patch "tiene como que la lentejuela" (has sequins) and expressing strong affection for the "super lindo" (super cute) unicorn pin. The background consistently shows a cluttered crafting table with various supplies, reinforcing the scrapbooking theme, all under bright, even lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a hand displaying a package of 'PINS AND IRON-ON PATCHES' with a pink geometric background, showcasing embroidered designs such as a red heart, blue envelope, yellow star, and a unicorn with a rainbow. As the hand rotates the package to reveal more colorful patches, including a red star and a flower, the speaker clarifies, "Los de arriba son pins y los de abajo son como para la ropa." Subsequently, the hand proceeds to flip through several packages of 'PUFFY STICKERS,' with the speaker announcing, "me compré unas stickers... me traje yo estos tres sets," and revealing that "Cada sticker, cada set cuesta 56 centavos." The hand highlights one package featuring bold, colorful stickers with phrases like 'YEAH,' 'WHAT?,' 'LET'S GO!,' and 'PARTY,' along with images of a pencil, rainbow, planet, and paper airplane, which the speaker describes as having "tijeras, tiene un hashtag, tiene un avioncito de papel." Another package displays stickers with a grid background, including a roller skate, ice cream, lightning bolt, and speech bubbles, with the speaker pointing out "un hashtag, varios textos, unos patines, una paleta de nieve, unos lentes." Throughout the segment, the background remains a cluttered crafting table filled with various scrapbooking supplies, all under bright and even lighting.

The speaker, in Spanish, describes these as "puffy stickers" that are "muy lindos" (very cute) and suitable for decorating, noting some have "sentimientos" (sentiment) themes, and reveals she purchased "tres por 56 centavos" (three for 56 cents). The hand then transitions to displaying several packages of 'Clear Stamps - Cutting Die' sets, each featuring intricate floral and leaf patterns, butterflies, and other botanical designs. The speaker identifies these as "nuevos" (new) stamps, explaining she is drawn to "los colores" (the colors), particularly pink, and highlights that each set includes both the stamp and a matching "troquel" (die-cut). She points to specific elements within the clear stamp packages, emphasizing their detailed outlines, and describes one set as "de flores" (of flowers), speculating they might be "girasoles" (sunflowers), and another as "de mariposas" (of butterflies) with its corresponding die-cut, stating each set cost "1.95 centavos" (likely 1.95 euros). The background remains consistent with other crafting materials, reinforcing the theme of a well-stocked scrapbooking workspace, while the hand’s deliberate movements and the speaker's commentary suggest a detailed product demonstration or review.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a human hand, entering from the bottom left, holding and rotating various packages of 'Clear Stamps - Cutting Die' sets against a backdrop of a cluttered crafting table filled with other scrapbooking supplies. The speaker, in Spanish, immediately focuses on a butterfly-themed set, remarking, "I think this one goes with this one," and clarifying that other designs "don't go with that butterfly." The hand then transitions to a floral-themed set, highlighting detailed flowers and leaves, which the speaker describes as "this other one that is also of flowers," specifically praising, "These two little branches are super cute." Moving on, a unicorn-themed set is presented, prompting the speaker to enthusiastically declare, "This one is super cute and this one is super cute!" She expresses reservations about some designs appearing "very dark to me, maybe I won't stamp them because it's too much ink," but still finds "these two are super cute." She then ponders the matching die-cuts, noting, "I don't know if this one matches... I'm not very sure," and specifically, "It doesn't bring a little unicorn die-cut." She also mentions, "Of this one I bought two of unicorns." Finally, the hand displays a cat-themed set, featuring black cat silhouettes, a striped cat, and a playful kitten, eliciting an excited, "And I saw it and I, oh, a kitten!" as she again considers the matching die-cut for the cat.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a human hand holding a transparent embossing folder, labeled 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS,' showcasing its textured grid pattern. As the hand rotates the folder to display its design, a female voice is heard speaking Spanish, discussing "our tutorials" and "recommendations from the girls," followed by her saying, "I already die-cut this because I already opened it." The hand then places the folder on a cluttered crafting table filled with various scrapbooking supplies. The voice continues, "Oh, I had the sample here," accompanied by subtle rustling sounds as the hand picks up a bright yellow cardstock with a matching embossed grid pattern. The speaker explicitly mentions finding "a yellow paper, very yellow," confirming she has located the sample. The hand carefully aligns the transparent folder over the yellow cardstock, pressing down to emphasize the embossed texture, while the voice affirms, "And yes, it does the embossing very well." She explains that "of course, I had to put extra paper so it would be thicker, because this one is very thin," and clarifies that if not thick enough, it "won't die-cut anything" with "plates" used for embossing. The hand lifts the folder to reveal the crisp, raised pattern on the cardstock, running fingers over it to highlight the tactile quality. The speaker corrects herself, stating, "No, it's die-cut, I'm confusing the word. The relief is made," emphasizing the embossed "relief." The background remains consistent with a vibrant array of crafting materials, including clear stamps with unicorn and floral designs, colorful buttons, and patterned paper stacks, all arranged on a green cutting mat. The hand continues to manipulate the embossed cardstock, turning it to show the evenness of the texture from multiple angles, as the voice concludes that "it does work" and she "bought several sets" without trying them first. The scene maintains a bright, well-lit, and colorful crafting environment, emphasizing the tactile and visual appeal of the embossing process.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a transparent embossing folder, labeled 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS' by CRAFT sensations. A female voice speaking Spanish immediately begins, stating that "it cost me one euro, well, 99 cents, one euro, each set," and that "it brings its cardstock, its embossing folder, and three little stamps." As the hand rotates the first folder to display its grid pattern and clear stamps, the speaker reads "This one says 'better together'," confirming the phrase 'better together,' and identifies a butterfly and a framed floral motif, which she calls "a little square." She explains that "this stays inside here" and "if you stamp it here, well, it stays perfect." The hand then shifts to a second embossing folder, which the speaker introduces as "another one" that she hasn't opened, noting it "brings like little flowers." This folder features a textured pattern of small, raised dots, and clear stamps. The speaker points out a 'Birthday!' stamp, a bird, and "just a little frame." A third embossing folder is then shown, which the speaker describes as "bigger, much bigger." This folder has a polka-dot texture and stamps with a floral frame, a sunburst, and a circular 'Happy Birthday' design. The speaker confirms it "also brings stamps," identifying a 'Birthday' stamp, a flower, and "the little frame that matches." She further describes its texture as "like with dots." Throughout the segment, the hand continues to point at and highlight each stamp, showcasing the variety of designs available, while the background remains consistent with a brightly lit, cluttered crafting table filled with scrapbooking supplies, including clear stamp sets with unicorn and floral designs, colorful buttons, patterned paper packs, and embellishments, all arranged on a green cutting mat.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a hand holding an 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS' package. The speaker immediately introduces it in Spanish, saying "Compré este otro" (I bought this other one). As the hand rotates the transparent folder, showcasing its intricate embossed patterns, the speaker describes its clear stamp designs, noting one that "dice con amor el sellito" (says 'with love' on the little stamp), another with "una flor y un marquito para aquí adentro" (a flower and a little frame inside), and a pattern she calls "arabescos." The hand continues to manipulate the folder, revealing additional designs. The speaker then introduces a second folder, stating "por último me compré este" (finally I bought this one), explaining that "había todos los diferentes, me los traje" (there were all the different ones, I brought them). She struggles to translate a stamp reading "hoy mejor" (today better), admitting "No sé cómo traducirlo esto en español," but expresses her liking for another stamp that "dice correo" (it says 'post') and points out "un marquito" (a little frame) on the folder. The camera pans slightly to show a second embossing folder with a different pattern, which the speaker describes as having "flechitas y corazones" (little arrows and hearts), finding it "super lindo." She mentions she hasn't tried them yet ("no los he probado"), but explains her plan to use these folders to create a "más grueso el sándwich" (thicker sandwich) with multiple layers of paper or cardstock for embossing, confident it will work, reiterating "me compré estos embossing folders." The hand then places the embossing folders down and picks up a clear plastic bag filled with colorful buttons, labeled 'Buttons,' which the speaker generally refers to as "unas gemas que vi" (some gems that I saw) and "estos botes" (these jars/containers) she acquired. The hand moves the bag closer to the camera, highlighting the buttons' vibrant colors and variety. The segment concludes with the hand picking up a small plastic package labeled '4 FIGURE ERASERS,' containing four pineapple-shaped erasers, and rotating it to display all sides. Finally, the hand holds up a package of 'PUFFY STICKERS' with bold, colorful designs, pointing to specific stickers to emphasize their playful and vibrant nature. The background consistently features a green cutting mat and various scrapbooking supplies, maintaining a bright, crafting-focused atmosphere.

Todos sus colores veraniegos y y y figuras... Este está muy bonito, me gustó" (to me it seems, uh, like summer. All its summery colors and figures... This one is very pretty, I liked it).
